human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for air-cargo-specific coordination behavior, AWB and cargo status terminology, shipment custody and documentation handling, route and schedule compliance impact, customs, security, DG, dispatch, maintenance, and safety refusal boundaries, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to air cargo dependency intake, owner routing, handoff packaging, blocker tracking, custody and documentation gap escalation, route-impact summaries, and closure confirmation rather than generic logistics execution.

Authority sources cover CBP Air AMS and air commerce regulation, eCFR customs and air cargo data rules, PHMSA hazardous-materials-by-air rules, TSA indirect air carrier and certified cargo screening rules, IATA e-AWB, ONE Record, cargo handling, ULD references, and Cargo iQ milestone or quality-management terminology.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, missing prerequisites, duplicated ownership, custody and documentation breaks, customs boundary refusal, route disruption with compliance impact, execution-boundary rejection, dangerous-goods safety escalation, security screening refusal, low-confidence handling, conflicting policy escalation, and tenant terminology adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/air-transportation-and-airports/air-cargo-coordination-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent dispatch, customs/compliance, maintenance, warehouse, customer operations, cargo security, dangerous-goods, ramp, airline operations, and airport operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.
The package does not claim a retained implementation; runtime_strategy remains specification-based.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated air cargo coordination specialist for dependency intake, handoff packaging, blocker escalation, custody-aware routing, route-impact summaries, regulated-boundary refusals, and closure confirmation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
